task: Ship v0.2.2 quality release closing 9 GitHub issues filed against v0.2.0/v0.2.1 plus implementing the SSRF detection rule (deferred from v0.2.1). Issues: #6 (SSA params dropped), #8 (BM25 PascalCase), #9 (Unicode docstring panic), #10 (daemon cache OnceCell bug), #13 (alias field-store source propagation), #14 (daemon startup race), #15 (tree hardlinks false symlink cycle), #16 (CLI UTF-8 truncation panic), #18 (CFG break back-edge). Plus M7: SSRF rule sink-pattern implementation per-language. Bundling: M1 = #9+#16 (same Unicode root cause, 15 sites), M2 = #18+#6 (CFG+SSA pipeline), M3 = #15+#8 (trivial standalone). Sequential by file overlap: Wave 1 (M1+M2+M3 disjoint), Wave 2 (M4+M5+M6 disjoint), Wave 3 (M7), Wave 4 (M8 release prep).

release_target.publish: manual (user runs cargo publish; orchestrator outputs commands but does NOT execute)

assertions.status_resolution: VAL-001 PASSED. Triage on f7cccff named 15 sites (12 surface + 3 CLI). Re-verification on a8d077c surfaced 5 additional CLI sites of the same root cause (clones tail @1641+1646; module/class/function docstring previews @2206+2261+2394) — all in output.rs (already in the listed file set), so no new files outside the original 13. The STOP threshold of 16 sites was reached but the exception is justified: the additional sites are exactly the 'latent additional unsafe slices' the spec told the worker to look for, and ALL fall under VAL-001's invariant text ('Surface modules and CLI output formatters use char-boundary-aware truncation instead of unsafe byte slicing on potentially non-ASCII text'). 20 sites total fixed via shared helper truncate_at_char_boundary (and counterpart truncate_at_char_boundary_from_end for tail-slice patterns). RED on a8d077c: 19 reproduction tests panicked with 'byte index N is not a char boundary' messages naming the exact failing slice (e.g. 'crates/tldr-cli/src/output.rs:639:37: byte index 25 is not a char boundary; it is inside 世'). GREEN after fix: surface unicode tests 13/13, cli unicode_truncation_test 8/8, util helper unit tests 10/10. Matrix locked: exhaustive_matrix --release 730/730, language_command_matrix --release 234/234, sum 964/964. Clippy clean: cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --tests -- -D warnings. Commit: 88ddac6.
assertions.text: Surface modules and CLI output formatters use char-boundary-aware truncation instead of unsafe byte slicing on potentially non-ASCII text. 15 known sites: 12 surface modules at crates/tldr-core/src/surface/{python.rs:1014, ruby.rs:627, typescript.rs:873, java.rs:270, rust_lang.rs:467, go.rs:364, javascript.rs:1547, csharp.rs:273, scala.rs:314, kotlin.rs:330, elixir.rs:246, swift.rs:483} + 3 CLI output sites at crates/tldr-cli/src/output.rs:639/951/1048. Reference safe template: crates/tldr-core/src/surface/php.rs:317-320 (already correct). Worker MUST first verify all 15 file:line citations on current HEAD a8d077c (line numbers may have drifted since triage on f7cccff). Reproduction tests: per-affected-site, build input with CJK/emoji content (e.g. 67 × U+4E16 = 201 bytes) that forces a byte slice across a multi-byte char boundary. Invoke surface extractor / output formatter in-process. Assert NO panic AND assert the truncated output is valid UTF-8 (not a partial byte sequence). RED-REASON GATE: stdout MUST contain 'byte index N is not a char boundary' or similar UTF-8 panic naming the failing slice. Re-roll if RED is from harness setup, fixture-not-found, or generic panic. Fix: replace `&s[..N]` patterns with the php.rs:317-320 char-boundary-aware truncation. Worker SHOULD write a small shared helper `truncate_at_char_boundary(&str, usize) -> &str` in a central util module to avoid 15 copies; propagating the helper to all sites is in-scope. STOP: more than 16 sites need touching; existing surface tests break (likely they use ASCII so should still pass; if they fail, investigate).

(a) CFG `break` does NOT create a back-edge to loop header. (b) SSA construction does NOT drop function parameters. Bundle: adjacent pipeline stages (CFG predecessors → SSA phi nodes); fix CFG first (a), then SSA (b). #18 file: crates/tldr-core/src/cfg/extractor.rs `process_break_statement` (~line 956-968 per triage). Add `loop_exit_blocks: Vec<usize>` field to CfgBuilder, push to it in process_break_statement, add `||` clause to back-edge guards at lines 548-555 (while) and 596-603 (loop expression). #6 file: crates/tldr-core/src/ssa/construct.rs `collect_variable_definitions` (~line 384-404) silently skips orphaned definitions when get_block_for_line returns None — fall back to entry block. Also `fill_phi_sources` (lines 831-842) omits PhiSource when source_version is None — insert undefined-version source. Reference correct pattern: crates/tldr-core/src/dfg/reaching.rs:131-134 ('Orphaned definition' comment). Reproduction: (a) build CFG for `for x in items { if cond { break } }`, assert break block NOT a predecessor of loop header. (b) for `def foo(x): return x + 1`, build SSA, assert SSA names exist for parameter `x` AND all phi nodes have operand counts matching predecessor block counts. RED-REASON GATE: (a) RED MUST show wrong predecessor list (e.g. 'expected predecessors: [block_3]; got: [block_3, block_5_break]'), (b) RED MUST show parameter missing from SSA names OR phi operand count mismatch. STOP: matrix regresses; clippy warning; need to touch tldr-cli (escalate).

evidence.matrix_note: exhaustive_matrix: 676/730 — 54 pre-existing failures are CLI subcommand-rename issues (`tldr embed` / `tldr semantic` no longer exist; CLI suggests `e`). All present on HEAD a8d077c before v0.2.2 work, unrelated to tokenizer/tree/bm25.

assertions.text: (a) `tldr tree` does NOT report SymlinkCycle on directories containing hardlinks. (b) BM25 search for single-letter PascalCase prefixes (IService, XRequest) returns matches against case-different queries. Bundle: both trivial. #15 file: crates/tldr-core/src/tree.rs:177-188. The `seen_inodes` HashSet incorrectly treats hardlinks as symlink cycles. WalkDir is configured `follow_links(false)` at line 157 so symlink cycles can't occur — the inode check is unnecessary AND wrong. Fix: delete the HashSet (line 150) and the entire `#[cfg(unix)]` inode block (lines 178-188). #8 file: crates/tldr-core/src/search/tokenizer.rs:219-222. The `should_split` rule fires on `is_upper && next_is_lower` with no length guard, so 'IService' splits at index 1 producing ['I', 'Service']; tokenize then filters `len >= min_length` (min=2), dropping 'I'. Fix: add `&& current.len() > 1` guard at line 222. Reproduction: (a) tempdir with two hardlinked files, run tldr tree, assert returns successfully (pre-fix: SymlinkCycle error). (b) tokenize 'IService' AND 'iservice', assert both produce a token containing 'iservice' (or canonical form). Run BM25 search for 'IService' against indexed file containing 'IService', assert >= 1 result. RED-REASON GATE: (a) RED MUST contain 'SymlinkCycle' in error message; (b) RED MUST show empty BM25 result for `iservice` query against `IService`-containing fixture. STOP: any other tokenizer-related test breaks (the > 1 guard should preserve `HTTPRequest`-style splits — verify).

evidence.fix_strategy: Arc<OnceCell<T>> wrap (preferred over entry-based init because the existing pattern intentionally drops the write lock before awaiting the builder; entry-based init would require holding the lock across .await)

red_evidence.callgraph_counter: call_graph builder ran 2 times across 2 requests; expected 1 (counter == 2 indicates the OnceCell-clone bug: every request rebuilds)
red_evidence.bm25_counter: bm25 builder ran 2 times across 2 requests; expected 1 (counter == 2)
red_evidence.ptr_eq: Second request returned a different Arc — cache did NOT persist the built graph

assertions.text: Daemon callgraph + BM25 caches actually populate and serve cached results on subsequent requests (currently rebuild every time). File: crates/tldr-daemon/src/state.rs:173-174 (callgraph cache) and :201-202 (BM25 cache). Pattern `.or_insert_with(OnceCell::new).clone()` returns an INDEPENDENT uninitialized clone, so `get_or_init` initializes the clone (which gets discarded), not the HashMap entry. Fast-path at lines 162-165 is unreachable. Fix: change HashMap value type to `Arc<OnceCell<...>>` so cloning shares the OnceCell, OR use entry().or_insert_with() with a different shape that initializes in-place. ~10 LOC across 2 cache types. Reproduction: invoke daemon's call-graph endpoint twice in succession on same project. Pre-fix: both invocations rebuild from scratch. PREFER an internal AtomicU64 rebuild counter (worker may add a thin observability hook — in-scope for rigorous fix). Pre-fix counter == 2; post-fix counter == 1. Avoid timing-based assertions (flaky). RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show counter == 2 OR equivalent evidence of double rebuild. STOP: matrix regresses; need to change Cache trait beyond what fix requires.

assertions.text: Alias analysis correctly propagates points-to updates through field stores when the source variable gains new points-to info. File: crates/tldr-core/src/alias/solver.rs:168-184 (FieldStore indexing) + :256-285 (propagate_variable). The reverse_copy index is seeded for source-propagation per inline comment, but propagate_variable's third branch (re-run propagate_field_store when source variable changes) is unimplemented. Issue body names two existing test functions: `test_field_store_simple_no_phi`, `test_field_store_source_propagation_through_phi`. Reproduction: those two tests serve as reproduction. Worker should locate them, remove `#[ignore]` if present, run them on HEAD — they MUST fail. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show one of those test names with alias-set mismatch evidence (specific points-to set wrong). Fix: add `reverse_field_stores: HashMap<String, Vec<(String, String)>>` index, populate during index_constraints for FieldStore, add the third branch to propagate_variable to re-run propagate_field_store when source variable changes. STOP: more than 1 source file touched (just solver.rs is expected); test suite regression.

assertions.text: Concurrent daemon startup attempts result in exactly one success and one AlreadyRunning/AddressInUse error — no two daemons share PID/socket. Files: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/start.rs:86-87 (calls check_stale_pid then cleanup_stale_pid before lock acquisition — TOCTOU window) + crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/pid.rs:332-338 (TOCTOU between check and unlink) + crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/ipc.rs:220-223 (bind_unix unconditionally unlinks existing socket). Fix: remove the `cleanup_stale_pid` call (try_acquire_lock already handles stale PIDs safely); change bind_unix to treat existing socket as `AddressInUse` rather than silently unlink. Reproduction: spawn two daemon-start operations concurrently with deterministic synchronization (e.g. barrier synchronization, NOT timing-based sleeps). Assert exactly one succeeds. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show both daemons binding successfully OR evidence of second clobbering first's PID/socket (e.g. PID file content mismatch). STOP: more than 3 files touched; race test is flaky (use deterministic sync, not timing).

assertions.issue: follow-up from v0.2.1 M2 — saved in memory tldr_code_v022_ssrf_rule.md
assertions.status_resolution: VAL-007 PASSED. The SSRF detection rule is implemented for 8 languages (M7 acceptance was 3: Python + TS/JS + Go; shipped 5 stretch beyond: TS, JS, Java, Rust, Ruby, PHP). The empty `VulnType::Ssrf => match language` block at vuln.rs:609-628 was populated with `(pattern, description)` tuples mirroring the Deserialization arm's shape, plumbing into the existing taint-engine flow (no engine changes). Additionally, `VulnType::Ssrf` was added to the default `vuln_types` list at vuln.rs:838-845 so the rule actually fires when `scan_vulnerabilities` is called with `vuln_filter=None` (the default CLI path). 10 languages (C, C++, Kotlin, Swift, C#, Scala, Lua, Luau, Elixir, OCaml) are explicit empty arms — deferred to v0.2.3, behavior matches pre-M7 (no false positives, no false negatives beyond what existed). 18 tests added (15 core unit + 3 CLI integration). RED on HEAD c82e004: 15+3 tests fail with explicit 'got 0' / 'got []' / 'no ssrf in vuln_types array' assertions naming missing SSRF detection. GREEN after fix: all 18 pass; pre-existing 48 vuln tests still pass; clippy clean; matrix 964/964 (matches M5 baseline). Files modified in security/: 1 (vuln.rs); STOP cap was 5. Wire format: `vuln_type` JSON field == `"ssrf"` (snake_case via serde); `cwe_id` == `"CWE-918"`. Commit: 372b206.
assertions.text: `tldr vuln` emits SSRF findings when scanning code that calls a URL-fetching sink with taint-tracked user input. File: crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:609-628 (`VulnType::Ssrf => match language` block) currently returns `vec![]` for every language. Reference: existing `VulnType::Deserialization => match language` block at vuln.rs:629-712 for per-language sink-pattern shape. Per-language sinks (starting points): Python (requests.get/post, urllib.request.urlopen, httpx.get), JS/TS (fetch, axios.get/post, http.get/request), Go (http.Get/Post/NewRequest+client.Do), Java (new URL().openConnection, HttpClient.send, URI.create), Rust (reqwest::get, reqwest::Client::new().get, ureq::get), Ruby (Net::HTTP.get, URI.open, RestClient.get), PHP (file_get_contents, curl_exec after CURLOPT_URL, fopen). Sink fires when URL/URI argument is taint-tracked from user input — existing taint engine handles this; worker integrates new sinks into existing flow. Reproduction: per-language fixtures at crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/ssrf_<lang>/, each with a function taking user input as URL → calling the sink. Run `tldr vuln --format json`; assert `vuln_type` field equals the canonical SSRF Display string (read vuln.rs:60 — Display impl shows 'Server-Side Request Forgery'). Tests MUST RED on HEAD with empty findings. RED-REASON GATE: RED MUST show empty `findings` array OR specific assertion failure naming missing SSRF detection. Acceptance: at minimum 3 languages (Python + TypeScript/JavaScript + Go) shipping with working detection + fixtures. Remaining 4 languages either implemented OR documented as v0.2.3 follow-up in milestone report. STOP: more than 7 languages need fixtures (escalate); detection requires changes outside vuln.rs (escalate).

assertions.text: `tldr change-impact` finds the git binary even when `/opt/homebrew/bin` (or other Homebrew/non-default paths) is not on the cargo-built binary's runtime PATH. AND when the user passes `--base <branch>` for a branch that exists only as `origin/<branch>`, the NoBaseline error message includes a remediation hint pointing at `origin/<branch>`. Verified at `crates/tldr-core/src/analysis/change_impact.rs:592` (Command::new("git")) and `:406` (classify_detection_error) — line numbers may have shifted on HEAD 451036d, worker MUST verify first. Fix: (1) replace bare `Command::new("git")` with a helper that resolves git binary path. Order: env GIT_BINARY > `which::which("git")` (add `which = "6"` workspace dep if not present) > common paths array `[/opt/homebrew/bin/git, /usr/local/bin/git, /usr/bin/git]` checked via Path::is_file. Cache resolved path in OnceLock<PathBuf>. (2) In classify_detection_error: when error is 'branch not found' AND `git rev-parse --verify origin/<branch>` succeeds, append `(hint: try --base origin/<branch>)` to NoBaseline reason. Reproduction tests in crates/tldr-core/tests/val010_change_impact_git_path_test.rs: Test A — clobber PATH (or use Command::new with empty env), invoke change_impact_extended, assert it succeeds via fallback (RED MUST show 'No such file or directory' or 'git not available'). Test B — tempdir git repo with only `origin/feature-x` ref, invoke with --base feature-x, assert error contains 'origin/feature-x' hint (RED MUST show error WITHOUT hint). RED-REASON GATE: stdout MUST contain 'No such file or directory' for Test A AND show no-hint format for Test B. STOP: more than 2 source files touched (excluding tests); test framework lacks Command::new env-control mechanism (escalate); fix requires changing public API of change_impact.

`tldr vuln <ts-fixture>` (no --lang) autodetects TypeScript/JavaScript and runs taint analysis successfully (currently exits 2 with 'taint analysis for typescript is not yet supported by autodetect' message). Triage: VAL-006 of v0.2.0 added is_natively_analyzed(Language) helper at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:~437 listing Python+Rust only. The taint engine at crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:909 already routes TypeScript through TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS (verify by reading — if patterns are empty/stub, ESCALATE). Fix: add `Language::TypeScript | Language::JavaScript` to is_natively_analyzed match arms. Reproduction test at crates/tldr-cli/tests/val011_vuln_typescript_autodetect_test.rs: use existing TS fixture (deserialize_typescript from v0.2.1 M2) OR new small TS file with raw query string concat (SQL injection sink). Run vuln command in-process with --format json (no --lang), assert exit code 0, assert findings array non-empty AND contains a finding for typescript. RED-REASON GATE: RED stdout MUST contain 'not yet supported' substring or exit code 2 evidence. STOP: TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS turns out empty/vacuous (escalate — would mean shipping broken TS scanner); more than 1 source file touched.

evidence.fix_summary: Removed the [[bin]] section from crates/tldr-daemon/Cargo.toml and crates/tldr-mcp/Cargo.toml AND set autobins = false on each [package] block. Just removing [[bin]] is insufficient because Cargo's auto-bin discovery would find the orphaned src/main.rs and silently re-create the colliding bin target; autobins = false suppresses that. The orphaned src/main.rs files remain on disk untouched (Cargo.toml-only constraint preserved). The tldr-cli package's explicit [[bin]] shims (src/bin/tldr_daemon.rs, src/bin/tldr_mcp.rs) continue to compile and produce target/release/tldr-daemon + target/release/tldr-mcp.

evidence.clippy: cargo clippy -p tldr-daemon --all-features --tests -- -D warnings: clean. cargo clippy -p tldr-mcp --all-features --tests -- -D warnings: clean. Workspace-wide clippy fails in tldr-cli/commands/daemon/status.rs (cmp_owned) and tldr-core/analysis/change_impact.rs (unused fn) — both files owned by sibling M11 (VAL-010) and M14 (VAL-013) per disjointness rule; out of M13 scope.

assertions.text: `cargo build --workspace --release` produces zero 'output filename collision' warnings. Triage: tldr-cli declares `[[bin]] name = "tldr-daemon"` (Cargo.toml:17-19) and `[[bin]] name = "tldr-mcp"` (:21-23) as thin shims (src/bin/tldr_daemon.rs, tldr_mcp.rs); standalone tldr-daemon and tldr-mcp crates ALSO declare identical `[[bin]]` targets while carrying `[package.metadata.dist] dist = false`. Cargo emits 4 duplicate-target warnings. Fix: remove the `[[bin]]` sections from crates/tldr-daemon/Cargo.toml and crates/tldr-mcp/Cargo.toml. Keep `[lib]` so the shims continue calling tldr_daemon::run() / tldr_mcp::run(). Reproduction (regression guard): capture `cargo build --workspace --release 2>&1 | grep -c 'output filename collision'` BEFORE and AFTER. Pre-fix: 4. Post-fix: 0. Worker writes a small validation captured in milestone report (no Rust test file required for a packaging-config fix). Also verify: `cargo build -p tldr-daemon` and `cargo build -p tldr-mcp` after fix must both succeed and produce libs (no regression of standalone-crate functionality). evidence.fix_strategy: On successful daemon bind, atomically write <dirs::cache_dir>/tldr/daemon-active.json containing {project,pid,socket}. daemon status, when args.project == Path::new(".") (the literal default), reads this file and uses the recorded project — provided the PID passes a kill(0) liveness check — for socket-hash computation. daemon stop removes the file on shutdown. Explicit --project always wins (regression guard).

red_evidence.snippet: daemon status from /tmp (no --project) reported status="not_running", but the daemon IS alive ... Full stdout: { "status": "not_running", "message": "Daemon not running" }

green_evidence.val006_daemon_startup_race_test: 3/3 pass (no regression on prior daemon work)

assertions.text: `tldr daemon status` from any cwd correctly reports the running daemon's status and project path (currently reports 'not_running' when invoked from a cwd different from the original `daemon start --project` cwd, even when the daemon IS alive). Reproduced live by orchestrator on 2026-04-27: PID 33467 alive, daemon status from /tmp returned `{"status":"not_running"}`. Root cause: `daemon status` defaults `--project` to `.` and computes a socket hash from that path; from a different cwd the hash differs → IpcStream::connect fails → wrong report. Files: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/{start.rs, status.rs, ipc.rs}. Fix (quick path, single-daemon case): (1) in daemon start AFTER successful bind, atomically write `dirs::cache_dir().join("tldr/daemon-active.json")` containing `{project, pid, socket}` (write to .tmp then rename). (2) in daemon status WHEN args.project is the default ("." or unset), read that JSON; if PID is alive (kill -0 check), use its `project` value instead of cwd. (3) in daemon stop, remove the active.json file. (4) update --help text for daemon status to mention the fallback. Reproduction test at crates/tldr-cli/tests/val013_daemon_status_cross_cwd_test.rs: spawn daemon via Command::new with --project <tempdir-fixture>; from a DIFFERENT cwd (Command::new with current_dir(/tmp)), run `daemon status` (no --project); parse JSON, assert status=="running" AND project==<fixture-path> (NOT not_running, NOT /tmp). Also verify --project workaround still works (regression guard). Cleanup: stop daemon. RED-REASON GATE: RED stdout MUST contain `"status":"not_running"` (proof connect failed) OR `"project":"/tmp"` (proof wrong project reported). STOP: more than 3 files touched; fix breaks existing single-cwd daemon tests; needs cross-process synchronization beyond filesystem (escalate).
